summary: Work is energy transferred by a force acting over a distance, A = F·s. It is positive when the force pushes along the motion.
method: Identify the force doing the work and the displacement over which it acts.
Make sure the force is along the direction of motion (only that component does work).
Decide which quantity is unknown — work, force, or displacement.
Rearrange A = F·s for the unknown.
Substitute in SI units (N, m) so the work comes out in joules.

example.title: Pushing a crate across the floor
steps: The unknown is A, so use A = F·s.
